name: AXS 1h Expansion Failure Rotation v1
edge_mechanism: a fresh short-term expansion fails to sustain, reclaims value, and is traded back toward balance with heavy early profit-taking and a strict time stop
thesis: AXS 1h is the best structurally different lane for a denser mean-reversion expression. Instead of trying to continue noisy AXS expansions, this idea trades expansion failures once value is reclaimed. The management is intentionally front-loaded because the asset is noisy and the edge should come from early capture, not long runners.
invalidation_condition: If failed expansions are still too sparse, or if first-target capture is too weak to survive costs, the mechanism is invalid.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A fresh local expansion loses acceptance, reclaims value, and rotates back through balance before the next impulse.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Noisy 1h assets often generate false short-term expansions. Capturing the return to balance with front-loaded exits should be more robust than insisting on sustained continuation.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: AXS 1h is the best fit because it is the noisiest lane in the basket and should produce enough failed expansions to test this management-first reversal idea.

indicator_role_map.PLUS_DI_14/MINUS_DI_14: fresh expansion participation that later fails

pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: AXS may be so noisy that failed expansion and genuine continuation are indistinguishable, degrading first-target hit rate.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If first targets almost work but not enough, tighten holding time before changing the expansion-failure trigger.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A larger number of small wins and some quick stop-outs, with expectancy driven by density and aggressive early monetization.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: AXS 1h produces enough failed-expansion rotations to clear screen density and first-target hit rate is strong enough to survive costs.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-10 trades, or repeated entries that cannot reach the first partial before noise takes them out.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: low relative to the rest of the batch because failed expansions on AXS 1h should recur

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit lane for expansion-failure rotation with front-loaded profit-taking.
validation_basket.continue_if: AXS 1h shows enough failed expansions and first-target behavior clears costs.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: AXS 1h remains too noisy for even aggressive early exits to survive.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: High enough participation with many first-target captures and limited need for runners.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same failed-expansion logic survives on a cleaner primary lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 1h preserves the same return-to-balance structure with cleaner execution.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 1h confirms the idea is too dependent on AXS noise.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower count, cleaner failed-expansion rotations than AXS.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same management expression ports to a more liquid major.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC 1h preserves failed-expansion rotation with acceptable density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: BTC 1h is too structurally different and suppresses the setup.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower count, cleaner first-target behavior than AXS.

long: Exit remaining 20% on 4 bars or touch of EMA_20 + 0.25 * ATR_14
short: Exit remaining 20% on 4 bars or touch of EMA_20 - 0.25 * ATR_14
